name: Kubernetes Cluster Takeover
description: Attack tree modelling compromise of a Kubernetes cluster from initial access through API server exploitation, container escape, and full cluster admin takeover
root_objective: Gain cluster-admin privileges and control all workloads in a Kubernetes cluster

nodes.title: Achieve Kubernetes Cluster-Admin
nodes.description: Escalate privileges within a Kubernetes cluster to gain full control over all namespaces, workloads, and secrets

nodes.title: Exploit Exposed API Server
nodes.description: Directly access the Kubernetes API server exposed to the internet or internal network

nodes.title: Exploit Anonymous Authentication
nodes.description: Access the API server with anonymous auth enabled, granting read or write to cluster resources

nodes.title: Steal Kubeconfig from Developer
nodes.description: Obtain a kubeconfig file with cluster-admin credentials from a developer's laptop or CI/CD system

nodes.title: Compromise a Running Container
nodes.description: Gain code execution within a container running in the cluster

nodes.title: Exploit Vulnerable Application
nodes.description: Exploit an RCE vulnerability in a web application or microservice running in a pod

nodes.title: Exploit Malicious Container Image
nodes.description: Push a backdoored image to the container registry that gets deployed as part of normal CI/CD

nodes.title: Escape Container to Node
nodes.description: Break out of the container sandbox to the underlying Kubernetes node

nodes.title: Exploit Privileged Container
nodes.description: Abuse a container running with privileged: true or hostPID/hostNetwork to access the host

nodes.title: Mount Host Filesystem via HostPath
nodes.description: Container with hostPath volume mount can read/write the node's filesystem including kubelet credentials

nodes.title: Exploit Container Runtime Vulnerability
nodes.description: Use a container escape CVE (e.g., CVE-2019-5736 runc) to break out to the host

nodes.title: Abuse Service Account Token
nodes.description: Use the auto-mounted service account token to escalate privileges within the cluster

nodes.title: List Secrets with Over-Privileged SA
nodes.description: Use an overly-permissive service account to list and read all secrets in the namespace or cluster

nodes.title: Create Privileged Pod via SA
nodes.description: Use a service account with pod-create permissions to spawn a privileged pod for node access
